commands.review_summary:
The branch preserves claude-cli warm-stdin bindings after failed native transcript probes and forwards missing-transcript candidates into prepare-time raw reseed, with regression tests.

PR surface: Source +22, Tests +113. Total +135 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: current main clears the warm-stdin binding after a failed native transcript probe and drops the missing-transcript candidate before prepare can arm raw reseed; I did not run a live claude-cli session in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed from this review; maintainers need to review the session-state contract and wait for current-head CI/build proof.

target.title: fix(agents): recover claude-cli warm-stdin continuity when no native transcript is written (reseed candidate not dropped)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #96564. Persisting the claude-cli session binding across turns (what #96584 and #96579 did) is **necessary but not sufficient** — under the bug condition (a warm-stdin `claude-cli` turn where no native transcript is written) continuity still does not recover. The missing second half lives in `resolveReusableCliSessionBinding` (`src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts`), which on a missing transcript clears the store **and returns `undefined`**, starving `prepare`'s already-correct reseed of the candidate session id. This PR adds both halves, with a regression test that fails on `main` and passes with the fix.

Each half is a no-op alone; both are required.

## Root cause

The reseed-from-OpenClaw-transcript path in `prepare` is fully built and armed — it is simply never fed a candidate under the bug condition:

1. `resolveReusableCliSessionBinding` (`src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts`): when the provider is claude-cli, a binding exists, but `claudeCliSessionTranscriptHasContent(...)` is `false` (no native transcript), it logs `cli session reset … reason=transcript-missing`, calls `clearCliSessionInStore(...)`, then **`return undefined;`**.
2. That `undefined` is passed into `runCliAgent`/`prepare` as both `cliSessionId` and `cliSessionBinding`, so `reusableCliSessionCandidate` collapses to `{}` and the in-`prepare` missing-transcript detector never fires.
3. So `rawTranscriptReseedReason` stays `undefined`, and `loadCliSessionReseedMessages`'s `loadRawTail()` bails at its allowed-reason guard (the allowed set *does* include `missing-transcript`).
4. Net: `openClawHistoryPrompt` is empty → `historyPrompt=none` → cold start, even though the binding was perfectly persisted.

## The fix (two parts)

- **Part 1 — persist the binding on a warm-stdin post-turn flush-probe miss** (`src/agents/cli-runner.ts`, `buildCliRunResult` + an `isWarmStdin` check at the call site). This is the binding-persistence half pioneered in #96584 (credit @xydigit-zt) and #96579 (@liuhao1024). Needed so the next turn *has* a stored binding.
- **Part 2 — `resolveReusableCliSessionBinding` returns the binding instead of `undefined`** when the transcript is missing. The store is already cleared (so no stale `--resume` can leak to a later turn), but the candidate id now flows to `prepare`, which re-detects `missing-transcript`, keeps `useResume=false` (no phantom resume — this avoids the failure mode flagged on #96584's later prepare-time probe-skip), and arms raw-transcript reseed.

## Why a test didn't catch this

The existing cli test `does not pass --resume when the stored Claude CLI transcript is missing` **asserted the buggy behavior** — that `cliSessionId`/`cliSessionBinding` are `undefined` on a missing transcript. That codified the regression, which is why it shipped and the binding-persist-only PRs don't recover continuity. This PR updates that test to assert the corrected contract: the persisted **store** binding is still cleared (no stale `--resume`), but the candidate id rides along so reseed can recover.

## Tests (auth-free — no claude OAuth, no live process, no bundle)

Run with: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts`

- **Regression test** (`attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`): persisted binding + no native transcript on disk → asserts `runCliAgent` receives `cliSessionId = <bound id>` (not `undefined`). **Fails on `main`** (`expected undefined to be 'cli-sid-abc'`), passes with Part 2.
- **Companion** (`prepare.test.ts`): `missing-transcript` reason + `reseedFromRawTranscriptWhenUncompacted: true` → `reusableCliSession === { invalidatedReason: "missing-transcript" }` and `openClawHistoryPrompt` contains the prior conversation (reseed actually delivers). Complements `main`'s existing `#77011` test, which only asserts the invalidation reason, not reseed delivery.
- Both files green: **102 passed**.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** warm-stdin `claude-cli` (Max-subscription) loses multi-turn continuity when no native transcript is written — the flush-probe miss wipes the binding and reseed is starved, so every turn cold-starts.
- **Real environment tested:** live OpenClaw 2026.6.10 + this two-part fix; real Discord DM; Max-subscription claude-cli (`liveSession: "claude-stdio"`); `memory-core` enabled.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** restart the gateway with the fix; multi-turn DM using a randomly generated 3-word passphrase; inspect the `cli exec:` log fields and the agent workspace file mtimes.
- **Evidence after fix:** the recall turns logged `useResume=false session=none reuse=invalidated:missing-transcript historyPrompt=present`, with `promptChars` growing 76 → 388 → 446 (the prior conversation injected as reseed history); the passphrase was recalled. The agent workspace had **zero file writes during the test** (mtime check) and the passphrase was random, so the recall could only come from reseed — not from the memory subsystem. Unit-level: the regression test fails on `main` and passes with the fix; 102 tests green across the two files.
- **Observed result after fix:** multi-turn continuity restored via reseed under the no-native-transcript condition; the happy path (native transcript present) is unaffected (`useResume=true`).
- **What was not tested:** *why* a warm-stdin claude-cli session sometimes writes no native transcript at all (the trigger that puts a session into the bug condition) is not root-caused here — candidates include the deterministic slug-path resolution from #81048 vs the warm-stdin invocation path. This fix makes continuity robust **regardless** of that trigger (reseed covers the gap); eliminating the trigger is a separate, complementary improvement. The full dist bundle was not built locally (the `tsdown` bundler OOMs on the dev box); CI builds it.

## Relation to existing PRs

#96584 (now closed) and #96579 implement Part 1 only, so reseed is never armed and continuity does not recover under the bug condition — which is why their real-behavior proof was missing. This PR supplies the missing half (Part 2), the regression coverage, and live proof. Credit to @xydigit-zt and @liuhao1024 for the binding-persistence work.

Fixes #96564

---

*Investigation, two-part fix, and live before/after/control verification by Claude Opus 4.8, working with the repo owner.*
target.author: bradreaves
